papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74592-xThe development of catalytic systems for the enantioselective epoxidation of sterically hindered trisubstituted alkenes and the oxidation of sulfilimines to sulfoximines using molecular oxygen (O₂) as the terminal oxidant remains a formidable challenge. papers.title: Spatiotemporal modulation of surface texture for information encoding and object manipulation
papers.authors: Xiao Yang, Jay Sim, Ruike Renee Zhao
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74794-3Dynamic control of surface textures is limited by static or coarse-patterned approaches, restricting their application in programmable object manipulation and information encoding. This study achieved reversible, spatiotemporally programmable surface wrinkling on a photothermal liquid crystal elastomer bilayer, enabling precise, on-demand surface patterning, object propulsion, self-erasing information encoding, and reversible assembly processes through localized light stimuli.

papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74658-wAntibodies that target the stem region of the influenza haemagglutinin are sought because this region is conserved between different clades and subtypes of the virus. papers.title: Supermicro Delivers NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL4 End-to-End DCBBS Blueprint with Native FP64 Performance for Converged HPC and AI Infrastructure
papers.abstract: SAN JOSE, Calif. and HAMBURG, Germany, June 22, 2026 — Super Micro Computer, Inc. today introduced the Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) Blueprint for HPC based on the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL4 platform, announced at ISC 2026. Following Supermicro’s DCBBS Blueprints for NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 and NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8 introduced at Computex, […] The post Supermicro Delivers NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL4 End-to-End DCBBS Blueprint with Native FP64 Performance for Converged HPC and AI Infrastructure appeared first on HPCwire.
